response:
The probability that a randomly selected student from Group B has higher engagement than one from Group A requires analyzing the difference between two normal distributions. The difference variable D equals X_B minus X_A and follows a normal distribution with mean 5 and variance 244. The standard deviation of D is approximately 15.62. We calculate the z-score as 0 divided by 15.62 which equals 0. The cumulative probability for z equals 0 is 0.5 so the final probability is 0.5.

An explanation of the performance improvement intersection shows that Group A uses exponential growth while Group B uses logistic growth. The process involves setting the two functions equal to find the time t. At t equals 5 the logistic function reaches half its maximum value of 50. The exponential function at t equals 5 yields 50 times e to the power of 0.1 which is about 55.2. Since 55.2 is greater than 50 the curves must cross before t equals 5. Numerical methods show the intersection occurs near t equals 4.2. There is nothing to force an exact algebraic solution because the equation mixes exponential and logistic terms. The time t is approximately 4.2 weeks when both groups share the same performance level.
